scenario: Larkspur Bank is upgrading 3 production services from Camunda 8.7 to Camunda 8.8 and wants to streamline how their 20 developers consume the APIs. The platform team is evaluating whether to write raw HTTP calls themselves or adopt the officially supported clients and SDKs documented under Working with APIs and tools.
question: Which official Camunda 8.8 clients and SDKs are positioned in the Working with APIs and tools overview as the recommended way to consume Camunda 8 APIs?

options.text: Use the official Camunda clients and SDKs for Java, Spring, and Node.js, which simplify API usage and speed up development of applications that build, automate, and monitor Camunda 8 processes
Use only the official Camunda Java client; the Spring and Node.js clients are listed as community-supported and do not provide simplifications over calling the Camunda 8 REST APIs directly

Use only the Camunda Go and Python clients listed in the overview as the speed-up options for Camunda 8, while Java, Spring, and Node.js applications should call the REST APIs without a client library
Use any HTTP client of your choice without an official SDK; the Working with APIs and tools overview removes mention of official clients for Camunda 8.8 and treats raw REST as the recommended path

a.text: Correct. The Working with APIs and tools landing page describes Camunda 8 APIs and clients as the way to build, automate, and monitor applications, and points to official Camunda clients and SDKs for Java, Spring, and Node.js that simplify API usage and speed up development.

b.text: Incorrect. The page lists Java, Spring, and Node.js as official Camunda clients and SDKs that simplify API usage, not just Java with the others as community offerings.

c.text: Incorrect. Go and Python are not the languages called out in the overview; the named official clients and SDKs are Java, Spring, and Node.js.

d.text: Incorrect. The 8.8 page explicitly highlights official clients and SDKs as the recommended path, with a dedicated section for upgrading APIs and tools to 8.8.

explanation: The Camunda 8.8 Working with APIs and tools overview recommends the official Camunda clients and SDKs for Java, Spring, and Node.js as the way to simplify API usage and speed up development against the Camunda 8 APIs.
